creative_brief: Hero image: founder standing in a regenerative farm field at golden hour, looking directly at camera — warm, honest, unpolished. Below-the-fold sections use close-up photography of named supplier produce (Bundarra pork, Collins seafood) with farm-name captions. Earthy palette, no stock imagery. The page should feel like a letter, not a pitch deck.
headline: I spent 22 years pretending the food system worked
subhead: Then I started tracing what my family actually ate — where it grew, how far it travelled, who got paid. The answers changed everything I thought I knew about groceries.
hero_copy: For two decades I bought what the big chains told me was fresh. I trusted the labels. I fed my kids without asking questions. Then one afternoon I stood in Rachel Ward's farmhouse kitchen in Kempsey, watching her pull vegetables from soil she'd spent years regenerating — and I realised I'd never once met the person who grew my family's food. That's the moment FarmThru started. Not with a business plan. With a feeling I couldn't shake: we deserve to know.

sections.heading: The thing I couldn't unsee
sections.body: Rachel showed me what regenerative farming actually looks like. Soil that holds water. Land that gets healthier each season. Animals raised the way your grandparents would recognise. Then she told me what the big chains paid her neighbours for the same quality produce — pennies after the middlemen took their cut. I drove home through 400 kilometres of farmland and couldn't stop thinking: there are farmers doing extraordinary work, and families who'd pay fairly for it, and the only thing between them is a broken supply chain. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen. That became our rule. FarmThru connects you directly to farms like Bundarra (pork from NSW), Collins Wild Caught (South Coast seafood), Paris Creek Farms (carbon-neutral dairy from South Australia), and Farmer Brown (eggs, NSW). You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. The food is fresher because it hasn't spent weeks in cold storage. The farmers earn more because nobody skimmed the middle.

sections.heading: This isn't a subscription box — it's a grocery store with a conscience
sections.body: I built FarmThru as the shop I wished existed. A real online grocery store stocked with regenerative produce, priced for Tuesday-night dinners, not just special occasions. Every product traces back to a named farm. We'll show you where your food comes from — that's not a slogan, it's a promise printed on every order. Our hub-and-collect model keeps overhead low so we can pay farmers fairly and keep your weekly shop honest. No lock-in. No gimmicks. Just food we'd feed our own kids. Because that's exactly what we do.

sections.heading: We're building this together — and you can be part of it
sections.body: FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. That means everyday people — not just venture capitalists — can own a piece of what we're growing. I can't promise returns. I can't guarantee success. Building a new kind of grocery store is genuinely hard, and investing in any early-stage company carries real risk. But if you believe families deserve to know where their food comes from, and farmers deserve a fair price for growing it, I'd love you to join the waitlist. A refundable $5 deposit secures your spot and tells us you want in when the campaign goes live. No obligation. No pressure. Just a signal that you think food can work differently.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
